big surprise to the athletic world.


There will be international yacht
races, motor boat races. a roller
skating tournament open to the
whole world, clog dancing cham
pionships and practically every
form OF athletic contest known to
the calendar of sports.


Capt. William Inglis. manager of
the Seattle Athletic club. who went
to New York and secure dthe A. A.
U. games for the exposition, has
been elected one of the vice presi-
dents of the Amateur Athletic
union. and is In charge of arrange
ments for the carnival.


An immense stadium Is now be-
ing erected on the exposition
grounds at cost of $4,000, In
which all the field athetlics will be
held. The drills. Journeys aud con.
tests between uniformed compan-
les and organizations will also be
held In the stadium
